parent_message.text: Andy Soh It seems that Direct does not support menus that contain negative prices, which can be used in certain POS to remove an ingredient and pay less (i.e. Pizza, less cheese -0.50 EUR). Would it be a huge feat to add this feature? Thanks

messages.text: I don't think so. There will fundamental issue to this if we do not parse info correctly to the POS. And being an emulator/visualizer of our menu, this question would be to post to another team. However! We should be able to accommodate the default quantity on the modifier, so they can remove it and reduce the price. Would that work better?

Thanks Andy Soh I wouldn't venture into your suggestion, as much as I like it in principle. Negative prices come from the POS, so if we apply them to a menu and then send the orders with the same negative prices back to the POS, we are sort of "coherent" with what we received in the first place. If we implement an override like the one you suggested, some POS might not accept it (iPratico is an example).
Mali Parry-Owen Am I too cautious?
Oh wow! That's something new to me. Ideally, if Deliverect supports it, we should try our best to accommodate (I guess?)
But not too sure into this space of negative pricing. Will have to look further, i.e. prioritization, etc etc
No worries, just raising it since it popped out in a separate topic

yeah i mean, i think its crazy we stripped the ability. it is very rare to need it, i agree. but if the POS supports it and theres real usecases (i remember someone like Hakkassan used to sell a Premium Box for like £199 and then you could downgrade certain items to the non-premium options, so the customer was nudged to the more expensive, rather than have to actively upgrade), *and* we have our own channels that can/should support it, it should exist

Make sense but there are a lot of things being 'stripped off' one way or the other so I'm not sure if it's crazy. That said, does menu builder support it? Lorenzo Maresio can I trouble you to share an account for us to take a look?
v1 menu builder, no. v2.1 menu builder, also no (because thats just products)
the v1 menu builder message is correct though: [image attached]

Andy Soh Used our iPratico test account 6172a7213b2f31fda4499fec. I pushed a test menu with only one item (Pizza Asparagi) and a modifier (Scaglie di grana) with price -1.00 EUR. The menu push failed because a negative price was detected.
The behaviour of the menu builder is to accept negative prices when they come from the POS via sync (so they are "original" values) but reject a price override with a negative value.
In this case I created the product from scratch in iPratico (like a customer would), synced it with the Deliverect account and tried to push it with the menu to OO.

Hi team, there seems to be an issue with the customer data report that is exported from the social dash.

The `total_amount` that a customer has spent is missing a decimal, making it look like they have spent 100x what they actually have. Screenshots attached:
• Column E is what was provided by the original raw data download
• Column F is what the total amount should actually be, proven by the screenshot from the customer profile on the social dashboard

messages.text: Joey Cheung I believe that's accurate as it is numbers from a code standpoint. But you're right, we could have named the title differently to avoid confusion
but isn't this a customer facing document? If it's a customer facing file, it should be shown as how the customer will be reading it
Are there any plans to update this so that the numbers are correct from a 'non-coding' standpoint?

Joey Cheung, sorry, `total_amount` is the total of the smallest sub-unit of the currency.

i.e. we will be keep this as it's a wholesale generated from our database. this also means that if the user would want to import it back into an alternative system, there would be 0 conversion needed. it is unlikely that we would be changing to add a decimal as it would then be something we would have to process when exporting. at most, we can add a header saying that. total_amount would be displaying the smallest sub-unit of your account's currency.

Andy Soh just to share that I am with Joey here, and that is a problem that has been already solved back in Legacy. We have ISO tables that know and convert currencies with precision, following their decimal usage. If you want I can name the folks who solved this in the past
Hey team, do I understand correctly that the amount we don't show to customers / merchants the accurate amount customers have spent in their currency?

Hey Benjamin Mouflard.
If you buy a poke bowl for AED 40.90, in this report it will come as 4090
We are forcing the customer to add another column and divide by 100 (in the case of AED), to get a real value.
We understand that we save the values in the DB without the commas, but we should not be displaying them like that as well. This was something already done in Legacy:
To illustrate, here is a *correct* report, from the legacy dashboard, for Cali-Poke, downloaded just now:

Direct has a time out of 16 min for payment completion or else the order gets expired. Unfortunately, if the customer is still landed on the payment page they can still finalize the payment, which leads to an authorized payment but no order will be generated.
